what: bf16_persist page_size=128 for bs>=64 kv>=4096 only (keep ps=64 for bs<64)
why: ps=128 failed for bs<=32 (v102/v103) due to tolerance, but larger batches have more averaging. bs>=64 untested. Would halve page count for bs=64,256 kv=8k shapes.
override: Prior ps=128 attempts only tested bs<=32 where tolerance fails. This targets bs>=64 only, which has never been tested with ps=128. Larger batches have more KV tokens for averaging, reducing quantization noise per page.

what: a16w8 page_size=16 for bs=256 kv=1024 only (keep ps=8 for bs=32/64)
why: v95 tried ps=16 for bs=32/64,kv=1024 and failed tolerance at bs=64. But bs=256 has 8x more KV tokens for averaging, may not fail. Halves page count for bs=256 (32768->16384).
override: v95 tested bs=32/64 which failed at bs=64. This targets bs=256 ONLY which has never been tested with ps=16 for kv=1024. v32 also tested ps=16 but at different context (different base version, possibly different batch sizes). bs=256 has the most KV tokens of any shape.

what: bf16_persist ps=64 for bs=256,kv=1024 (replace a16w8 ps=8)
why: bf16_persist ps=64 is proven reliable and fast for kv>=4096. bs=256,kv=1024 has same token count (256K) as bs=32,kv=8192 which works perfectly with ps=64. Avoids fp8 quantization overhead.
override: Prior bf16_persist attempts for kv=1024 used small page_sizes (4/8/16/32). ps=64 has never been tested for kv=1024. It is proven reliable for kv>=4096 at all batch sizes including bs=256. The key insight is that ps=64 with bf16 precision has passed tolerance at 256K+ tokens, and bs=256,kv=1024 has exactly 256K tokens.

why: bf16_persist ps=64 with kv_granularity=64 means each work block handles exactly 1 page. With kv_granularity=32, each page generates 2 work blocks, allowing finer load balancing across CUs for the largest shape (bs=256,kv=8k).
override: Prior attempts tried kv_granularity=128 (LARGER than page_size). This tries kv_granularity=32 (SMALLER than page_size=64), which is the opposite direction -- more work blocks for finer scheduling. Only for bs>=256 to avoid regressing other shapes.

what_failed: Explored page_size and kv_granularity tuning for bf16_persist and a16w8 paths. ps=128 fails tolerance for all batch sizes. bf16_persist for kv=1024 fails tolerance at ps>=16. a16w8 ps=16 for bs=256 fails LB tolerance with recheck. kv_granularity=32 for bf16_persist bs>=256 is noise-neutral.
